tasks.instruction: Dopasuj nazwy brytyjskich tradycji lub świąt (1–6) do ich opisów (A–H). Dwa opisy zostały podane dodatkowo i nie pasują do żadnego elementu.

options.text: On this night, 5th November, people light bonfires and watch firework displays to remember the failed Gunpowder Plot of 1605.
This is a traditional afternoon meal consisting of sandwiches, scones with clotted cream and jam, and small cakes, served with tea.
On this day, 26th December, shops hold big sales and many people go shopping. It is also a bank holiday in the UK.
This annual race takes place on the River Thames between Oxford and Cambridge university rowing teams, usually in spring.
This ceremony happens every morning outside Buckingham Palace, when one group of soldiers officially takes over guard duty from another.
This is the night before 1st November when children dress in costumes, carve pumpkins, and go from door to door asking for sweets.
This popular annual event is a large outdoor music festival held in Somerset. It features hundreds of bands on many stages over several days.
This is a bank holiday in late August when a famous street carnival takes place in the Notting Hill area of London, celebrating Caribbean culture.
